sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Prologis는 2025년 부동산 부문 매출 81억 5,900만 달러를 기록하며 견고한 운영 성과를 달성했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 부동산 부문의 임대 수익은 81억 5,900만 달러로 전년 75억 1,500만 달러 대비 증가하며 핵심 사업의 성장세를 견인했습니다.
개발 포트폴리오 내 77개 자산에 대해 30억 달러를 투자 중이며, 완공 시 총 투자액은 51억 달러에 이를 전망입니다.

riskChanges.text: 글로벌 운영과 관련하여 지정학적 리스크 및 해외 사업장의 인력 관리, 노동 이슈가 재무 상태에 부정적 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.
사이버 보안 공격, AI 관련 리스크, 자연재해 등 시스템 장애가 발생할 경우 운영 중단 및 복구 비용이 발생할 위험이 있습니다.

businessChanges.text: 부동산 부문의 임대 비용은 19억 6,400만 달러로 전년 17억 6,500만 달러 대비 증가하며 운영 비용 부담이 확대되었습니다.
해외 사업장에서 발생한 매출은 7억 8,800만 달러로 전체 연결 매출의 9.0%를 차지하며 글로벌 다각화 전략을 유지하고 있습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025 회계연도 매출은 87억 9,012만 달러, 영업이익은 43억 5,786만 달러를 기록했습니다.
순이익은 33억 2,823만 달러이며, 총자산 987억 2,425만 달러와 현금 11억 4,564만 달러를 보유하여 안정적인 재무 구조를 유지 중입니다.

watchItems.text: 전략적 자본 부문의 NOI 감소와 부동산 부문의 비용 증가 추세가 향후 수익성 개선에 미칠 영향을 면밀히 모니터링할 필요가 있습니다.

FY2025 operating income = 43억 5,786만 달러 (USD 4.358B; 4,358 million), XBRL range 2025-01-01..2025-12-31.
FY2025 net income = 33억 2,823만 달러 (USD 3.328B; 3,328 million), XBRL range 2025-01-01..2025-12-31.
FY2025 total assets = 987억 2,425만 달러 (USD 98.724B; 98,724 million), XBRL range instant..2025-12-31.
FY2025 cash and cash equivalents = 11억 4,564만 달러 (USD 1.146B; 1,146 million), XBRL range instant..2025-12-31.

Risks Related to our Global Operations As a global company, we are subject to social, geopolitical and economic risks associated with conducting business in many countries and our results of operations and financial condition may be materially and adversely affected. We conduct a significant portion of our business and employ a substantial number of people outside of the U.S. During 2025, we generated approximately $788 million, or 9.0% of our consolidated revenues, from operations outside the U.S. Circumstances and developments related to international operations that could negatively impact us include, but are not limited to, the following factors: • difficulties and costs of staffing and managing international operations in certain geographies, including differing employment practices and labor issues; • local businesses and cultural factors that differ from our domestic standards and  Normalized numeric aliases: $788 million = 7억 8,800만 달러; $788 = 788달러.

Our business and operations could suffer in the event of system failures, cybersecurity attacks or risks associated with AI. Despite system redundancy, the implementation of security measures and the existence of a disaster recovery plan for our internal and hosted information technology systems, our systems are vulnerable to damages from any number of sources, including energy blackouts, natural disasters, terrorism, war, adverse impacts of AI, telecommunication failures and cybersecurity attacks, such as malware, ransomware, or unauthorized access. Any system failure or accident that causes interruptions in our operations could result in a material disruption to our business. We may incur additional costs for remediation caused by such disruptions. Third-party security events at vendors, sub-processors, and service providers could also impact our data and operations through unauthorize

Overview We believe our ability to generate cash from operating activities, distributions from our co-investment ventures, contributions and dispositions of properties and available financing sources provides sufficient capacity to meet our anticipated future development, acquisition, operating, debt service, dividend and distribution requirements. 38 Table of Contents Near-Term Principal Cash Sources and Uses In addition to dividends and distributions, we expect our primary cash needs will consist of the following: • completion of the development and leasing of the properties in our consolidated development portfolio (at December 31, 2025, 77 properties in our development portfolio were 53.5% leased with a current investment of $3.0 billion and a TEI of $5.1 billion when completed and leased, leaving $2.1 billion of estimated additional required i  Normalized numeric aliases: $3.0 billion = 30억 달러; $5.1 billion = 51억 달러; $2.1 billion = 21억 달러; $3.0 = 3.0달러; $5.1 = 5.1달러; $2.1 = 2.1달러.
